rag_rules: Place AGREE annex blocks in component type scopes for interface-level assumptions and guarantees, and in implementation scopes for subcomponent bindings.
Use only visible identifiers from the AADL model to avoid introducing undefined references.
generic_rules: Component type contracts should define assumptions and guarantees using visible ports and constants.
Implementation-level AGREE clauses must reference valid subcomponents and connections.
Do not introduce package-qualified names or types not present in the input AADL model.
Preserve AADL architecture structure during AGREE annex insertion.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Assumptions and guarantees must be placed in valid AADL scopes (type vs implementation).
anti_patterns: Avoid placing implementation-specific bindings in component type annexes.
Do not reference non-visible or undefined identifiers in AGREE annexes.
irrelevant_topics: AGREE syntax and annex placement rules
